sections.title: STANDARD FOR WHEY CHEESES
sections.text:
Formerly CODEX STAN A-7-1971. Adopted in 1971. Revised in 1999, 2006.
Amended in 2010, 2018, 2022.

STANDARD FOR WHEY CHEESES
level=1

sections.title: 2022 AMENDMENT
sections.text: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
sections.retrieval_text:
2022 AMENDMENT
level=1
The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

page_spans.text: The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty -fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

sections.title: AMENDMENT DETAILS
sections.text: Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version   4 Section 7.3 Labelling of non-retail containers  Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
sections.retrieval_text:
AMENDMENT DETAILS
level=1
Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version   4 Section 7.3 Labelling of non-retail containers  Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

page_spans.text: Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version   4 Section 7.3 Labelling of non-retail containers  Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to all products intended for direct consumption or further processing, in conformity with the definition of whey cheeses in Section 2 of this standard. Subject to the provisions of this standard, Codex standards for individual varieties of whey cheeses may contain provisions which are more specific than those in this standard.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Food additives listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in Food Category 01.6.3 (Whey cheese) and 01.6.6 (Whey protein cheese) may be used in foods subject to this standard.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995).

sections.title: Whey Cheeses are solid, semi -solid, or soft products which are principally obtained through either of the following processes:
path_titles: Whey Cheeses are solid, semi -solid, or soft products which are principally obtained through either of the following processes:
sections.text: (1) the concentration of whey and the moulding of the concentrated product; (2) the coagulation of whey by heat with or without the addition of acid. In each case, the whey may be pre -concentrated prior to the further concentration of whey or coagulation of the whey proteins. The process may also include the addition of milk, cream, or other raw materials of milk origin before or after concentration or coagulation. The ratio of whey protein to casein in the product obtained through the coagulation of whey shall be distinctly higher than that of milk. The product obtained through the coagulation of whey may either be ripened or unrip ened.

sections.title: Whey Cheese obtained through the concentration of whey
path_titles: Whey Cheese obtained through the concentration of whey
sections.text: is produced by heat evaporation of whey, or a mixture of whey and milk, cream, or other raw materials of milk origin, to a concentration enabling the final cheese to obtain a stable shape. Due to their relatively high lactose content these cheeses are typically yellowish to brown in colour and possess a sweet, cooked, or caramelized flavour.

sections.title: Raw materials
sections.text: (1) For products obtained through the concentration of whey: whey, cream, milk and other raw materials obtained from milk. (2) For products obtained through the coagulation of whey: whey, milk, cream and buttermilk.

sections.title: Permitted ingredients

sections.title: Permitted nutrients
sections.text: Where allowed in accordance with the General Principles for the Addition of Essential Nutrients to Foods (CXG 9-1987), maximum and minimum levels for minerals and other nutrients, where appropriate, should be laid down by national legislation in accordance with the needs of individual country including, where appropriate, the prohibition of the use of particular nutrients.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: METHODS OF SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name of the food shall be whey cheese. Where it is considered necessary for consumer information in the country of sale, a description of the nature of the product may be required. The words “whey cheese” may be omitted in the designation of an individual whey cheese variety reserved by a Codex standard for individual cheeses, and, in the absence thereof, a variety name specified in the national legislation of the country in which the product is sold, provided that the omission does not create an erroneous impression regarding the character of the food. In case a whey cheese obtained through the co-agulation of whey is not designated by a variety name, but with the designation “whey cheese”, the designation may be accompanied by a descriptive term such as provided for in Section 7.1.1 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). Unripened whey cheese obtained through the concentration of whey may be designated according to the fat content as provided in Section 7.2.

page_spans.text: The name of the food shall be whey cheese. Where it is considered necessary for consumer information in the country of sale, a description of the nature of the product may be required. The words “whey cheese” may be omitted in the designation of an individual whey cheese variety reserved by a Codex standard for individual cheeses, and, in the absence thereof, a variety name specified in the national legislation of the country in which the product is sold, provided that the omission does not create an erroneous impression regarding the character of the food. In case a whey cheese obtained through the co-agulation of whey is not designated by a variety name, but with the designation “whey cheese”, the designation may be accompanied by a descriptive term such as provided for in Section 7.1.1 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). Unripened whey cheese obtained through the concentration of whey may be designated according to the fat content as provided in Section 7.2.

sections.title: Declaration of milk fat content
sections.text: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated. For cheeses obtained from the concentration of whey, the declaration of milk fat content may be combined with an indication of the fat content as follows: Fat on the dry basis1 Creamed whey cheese minimum 33% Whey cheese minimum 10% and less than 33% Skimmed whey cheese less than 10%

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sentences.original: The words “whey cheese” may be omitted in the designation of an individual whey cheese variety reserved by a Codex standard for individual cheeses, and, in the absence thereof, a variety name specified in the national legislation of the country in which the product is sold, provided that the omission does not create an erroneous impression regarding the character of the food.

classification.sentence: Unripened whey cheese obtained through the concentration of whey may be designated according to the fat content as provided in Section 7.2.
classification.function: Permission to designate based on fat content
classification.pattern: Subject + permission modal + designation criteria + reference

components.subject: Unripened whey cheese obtained through the concentration of whey
components.permission: may be designated according to the fat content as provided in Section 7.2

classification.justification: Sentence expresses a permission tied to designation criteria, fitting partially a standard template with some adaptation due to reference and subject specifics.

sentences.original: In case a whey cheese obtained through the co-agulation of whey is not designated by a variety name, but with the designation “whey cheese”, the designation may be accompanied by a descriptive term such as provided for in Section 7.1.1 of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978).

sentences.original: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated.

classification.sentence: For cheeses obtained from the concentration of whey, the declaration of milk fat content may be combined with an indication of the fat content as follows: Fat on the dry basis1 Creamed whey cheese minimum 33% Whey cheese minimum 10% and less than 33% Skimmed whey cheese less than 10%
classification.function: Permission to combine declarations with indications listing minimum and maximum content
classification.pattern: Prepositional phrase + permission modal + combined declaration + enumerated quantitative specifications

components.condition: For cheeses obtained from the concentration of whey
components.permission: the declaration of milk fat content may be combined with an indication of the fat content
components.specifications: Creamed whey cheese minimum 33%, Whey cheese minimum 10% and less than 33%, Skimmed whey cheese less than 10%

classification.justification: Permission to combine declarations plus detailed enumerated quantitative content represents a new pattern beyond existing templates.
notes: Enumerated quantitative specifications within permission context

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR WHEY CHEESES
pages.summary: Página inicial do documento com título principal e informação histórica resumida.

pages.title: CXS 284-1971
pages.summary: Página apresenta o título do documento e página no cabeçalho. Identifica dois nós principais: '2022 AMENDMENT' como título em caixa alta, e o texto detalhado da emenda (sem título explícito claro, mas agrupado como 'AMENDMENT DETAILS'). O texto está completo nesta página, sem continuidade para outras páginas.

Página apresenta hierarquia clara com títulos numerados de 1 a 5 no nível 1, e subtítulos numerados no padrão 2.x e 3.x no nível 2 sob os títulos 2 e 3. Texto associado está estruturado respeitando a hierarquia e segue padrão típico de normas técnicas. Não há indicações de continuidade para outras páginas.

Página apresenta títulos principais numerados 6 a 8 no nível 1, com subtítulos numerados 7.1 a 7.3 no nível 2 sob o título 7 LABELLING. O texto está associado corretamente aos respectivos nós. Não há indicações de continuidade para outras páginas.
